title: Signature header parser does not handle escaped quotes
description: In inbox.rs parse_signature_header (line 208), the close-quote finder uses remaining.find('"') which will split at the first unescaped OR escaped double-quote. If a remote implementation sends a Signature value containing a backslash-escaped quote (e.g. in a keyId URL containing %22 that got decoded, or a pathological value), the parser truncates the value at the wrong position. The consequence is either: (1) signature verification fails (DoS for legitimate Move/Follow from that server), or (2) if the truncated keyId happens to match a cached key, signature verification against the wrong key (fail-safe, but confusing error). This is a robustness issue rather than a security hole because the worst case is a rejected message, but it will generate repeating issue reports from users on certain implementations that include unusual characters in keyId URIs.
